title: ABS Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The ABS( ) function allows you to return the absolute value of a numeric expression.

## Syntax

ABS(numeric_expr)

## Metasymbol

numeric_expr
An expression that is an integer or real number.

## Examples

In this example, if Y = -10, then X = 10.

X = ABS(Y)

body_preview:
BNOT () returns an integer expression that is the result of each bit in the base-2 number of the integer being reversed. This means that the BNOT( ) function changes all one bits to zeros, and zero bits to ones. Additionally, BNOT( ) changes the sign bit. If the value of the sign bit is zero, the integer is positive. If the sign bit value is one, the integer is negative.

## Syntax

BNOT(integer)

title: CALL LINE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: The CALL LINE command draws a vertical or horizontal line on a display. If the VERTICAL or XVERTICAL option is _not_ used, a horizontal line is drawn by default. Horizontal lines are always drawn from left to right and vertical lines are always drawn from top to bottom; be sure to provide the starting coordinate (begin_row and begin_col). The XVERTICAL option draws an extended vertical line that contains a crossbar a...

title: CALL PARSER Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CALL PARSER command parses the contents of a dynamic Discern Explorer command. Consecutive CALL PARSER commands terminated by a "GO" can be issued. Before Discern Explorer version 8.7.2 the CALL PARSER command was limited to 32,000 characters. Discern Explorer version 8.7.2 increased the limit to 100,000 characters.

## Syntax

CALL PARSER (expression [,mode])

title: CALL PAUSE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: The CALL PAUSE command causes a program to wait for the defined number of seconds. This slows the display so users can read messages or watch information placed on the screen before other CALL commands refreshes or clears it. CALL PAUSE should only be used in interactive back end Discern Explorer Screen Application programs. Using CALL PAUSE in programs that run through servers creates inefficient delays in the progr...

title: CALL PRINT Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CALL PRINT command prints the ASCII result of an expression. For the BUILD( ), CALCPOS( ), and TRIM( ) functions, the current position is left at the end of the variable-length result. This command is valid only in the Reportwriter section of a program.

## Syntax

CALL PRINT (expression)

## Metasymbol

_expression_
An expression is the highest-level metasymbol among constants, field names, variables, and so on....

title: CALL SCROLLDOWN Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CALL SCROLLDOWN command controls the way a scroll window reacts when a user presses the DOWN arrow. CALL SCROLLDOWN determines whether Discern Explorer advances to the next line in the scroll box or moves the top line of the scroll window out of the box and moves the next available line not in the scroll window to the bottom of the box.

## Syntax

CALL SCROLLDOWN (previous_row,current_row,expression)

title: CALL SCROLLINIT Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CALL SCROLLINIT command tells Discern Explorer that a scroll window is used in the display. To change between multiple scroll windows, issue a CALL SCROLLINIT command to establish the current scroll window followed by CALL SCROLLTEXT to display text to the current scroll window. Use the CALL BOX command to display a box around the scroll window.

title: CALL SCROLLTEXT Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CALL SCROLLTEXT command displays information on a row in a scroll window in normal video mode. For screen displays, normal mode is bold letters (intense) displayed in the default font and pitch for your terminal. The most common use of this command is to refresh a scroll window with new information once the PF1 or NEXT SCREEN key is pressed.

## Syntax

CALL SCROLLTEXT (begin_row,expression)

title: CALL SCROLLUP Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CALL SCROLLUP command controls the way a scroll window reacts when a user presses the UP arrow. This command determines whether Discern Explorer backs up to the previous line in the scroll box, or moves the bottom line of the scroll window out of the box and moves the next available line not in the scroll window to the top of the box.

## Syntax

CALL SCROLLUP (previous_row,current_row,expression)

# Overview

CCLLINK is the routine used to build a link to another Cerner Command Language (CCL) program within an .HTML file. When clicking the link, the program runs with the provided prompt values and is displayed in Discern Output Viewer (DiscernOutputViewer.ocx). This is useful for building report menus or linking one report to another report.

CCLORAINDEX displays a list of unique and nonunique index and column information in one table. You may find that CCLORATABLE is a more complete table dictionary tool showing both indexes and column names.

**Using CCLORAINDEX**

To access CCLORAINDEX, enter **CCLORAINDEX GO** at the Discern Explorer command line. The CCLORAINDEX prompt screen is displayed with the cursor in the ORACLE SYSTEM TABLES field.

**ORACLE S...

title: CCLORAPLAN Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
CCLORAPLAN generates an RDBMS plan outlining the path that was followed for all programs run by a particular user. Use this program to identify what indexes the RDBMS used to access the data in the SQL statement and what table access path was used. Complete the following steps to initialize CCLORAPLAN:

title: CCLORATABLE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
Use the CCLORATABLE program to generate a data dictionary that displays the indexes and columns on a table.

1. Run the command **CCLORATABLE GO**.
2. Press **ENTER**.
3. At the first prompt, press **ENTER** to accept the default MINE.
4. Enter **PERSON** at the second prompt (table name).
5. Press **ENTER**. The output from the CCLORATABLE program is displayed as a list of the unique and non-unique indexes (or keys)...

title: CCLPARSE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: CCLPARSE is a run-time routine that passes a whole or partial command to Discern Explorer. CCLPARSE reads the command buffer to a null character that terminates the command or to CCLSTRLEN characters. As with an interactive session of Discern Explorer, the commands are buffered until a terminating GO is encountered. Any characters in the command buffer following GO are ignored. CCLSTRLEN is defined in the header file...

title: CCLPARSE2 Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: CCLPARSE2 is a run-time routine that is used to pass a whole or partial command to Discern Explorer (CCL). The string is read to either a null character that terminates the command or to command_buffer_len. As with an interactive session of CCL, the commands are buffered until a terminating **GO** is received. Any characters following a **GO** are ignored in the command buffer. CCLSTRLEN is defined in the copybook CC...

title: CCLPROT Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: CCLPROT generates a list of the objects in the object library. The output also includes the username of the object's owner, the location of the source code that was used to create the object, the date and time the object was created, and the protection defined for each Discern Explorer group that has access to the object. Objects are listed in alphabetic order by type and name. The list can be displayed on the screen...

title: CCLPROTNODE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: The CCLPROTNODE program generates a list of the objects in the object library. In multinode environments, the object library is unique to each node. Use CCLPROTNODE to search the object library on one or more nodes in the environment. The output includes the object name, node name, the username of the object's owner, the location of the source code that was used to create the object, the date and time the object was...

title: CCLQUERY Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
CCLQUERY generates a report for a specific Discern Explorer program. The report contains the following sections:

1. Information about the compiled program is displayed in the first section.
2. The anticipated RDBMS PLAN statement for the queries in the program is displayed in the second section. The PLAN statement displays the anticipated order in which the tables are read, and the indexes used to read the tables. F...

title: CCLRECDEF Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The Record Definition Report (CCLRECDEF) generates a report that lists the record definition for one or more records in a file. CCLRECDEF can be run for a single file, multiple files, or all files. The list can be displayed on the screen, stored in a file, or printed at a designated printer.

**Using CCLRECDEF**

To access this program, enter **CCLRECDEF GO** at the Discern Explorer command line. The CCLRECDEF prompt...

title: CCLREFLOG Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: Oracle uses the CCLREFLOG symbol internally to track changes to code value tables in an environment. When the logical is set to **Y** , changes to the tables CODE_VALUE, CODE_SET, and CODE_ALIAS are logged to the CCL_REF_LOGGING table. This logging is not automatically turned on. If logging is turned on, the Discern Explorer utility program CCLREFLOG displays the logging information by table.

(The buffer to receive the results is specified immediately after the routine call.) For performance reasons, use CCLSET_UAR or CCLSET_WORK in place of this routine to return results to the user's program.

title: CCLSCRATCH Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: By default, the CCLUSERDIR directory is used for all files. This includes run-time server logs, temporary sort files, session history files, and any data files created by programs that are not specified to write to another location. If the disk space limit of CCLUSERDIR is filled, application issues result. A group level CCLSCRATCH logical can be configured to redirect temporary files to another location.

title: CCLSET Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
CCLSET run-time routines set various interface options for the calling environment. Typically, only more complex application programs make this call.

CCLSET has the following separate options that can be set:

CCLSET_CCLAPP
CCLSET_CLIENTCALLOC
CCLSET_CLIENTCFREE
CCLSET_CLIENTFREE
CCLSET_CLIENTMALLOC
CCLSET_CLIENTREALLOC
CCLSET_COMMIT
CCLSET_DBRESET
CCLSET_DBSET
CCLSET_EKSRETURN
CCLSET_ERRHANDLE
CCLSET_MOD
CCLSET_PRO...

title: CCLSET_CCLAPP Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
CCLAPP is a CCLSET run-time routine option that is used to indicate to CCL that this is a CCL application.

**Syntax**

int CCLSET(CCLSET_CCLAPP, int_option_, 0, 0, 0);

**Arguments**

_option_

* **Type:** Longword (signed)
* **Access:** Read only
* **Mechanism:** By value

If the option is set to **1** , then this is a CCL application. If the option is not set to **1** , then this is not a CCL application.

title: CCLSET_CLIENTCALLOC Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:

void *CCLSET(CCLSET_CLIENTCALLOC, int_number,_int_size_, 0, 0);

**Arguments**

_number_

The number of objects to allocate.

* Type: longword (signed)
* Access: read only
* Mechanism: by value

_size_

The size in characters to allocate memory for an object.

_Ptr_

* **INSERT:** Number of records inserted
* **SELECT:** Number of records output
* **UPDATE:** Number of records updated

title: CCLSOURCE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: Internally, Oracle stores production Discern Explorer source code in the CCLSOURCE directory. Oracle does not distribute Discern Explorer source code during an installation. The executable object is distributed through the Discern Explorer dictionary. Therefore, in a client Millennium Platform environment, only a small number of system files exist in the CCLSOURCE directory.

title: CCLUAF Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
CCL User (CCLUAF) generates a list that includes each Discern Explorer user ID and the Discern Explorer group to which the user belongs. By default, all users are assigned to Group 1. The list can be displayed on the screen, stored in a file, or printed at a designated printer.

title: CCLUSERDIR Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CCLUSERDIR directory is the default directory in Discern Explorer in which all files are placed unless you specify otherwise. This directory also contains the output files that are created using the SELECT INTO command unless you specify otherwise.

Log files and temporary files that are created and used by Discern Explorer programs also are stored in the CCLUSERDIR directory.

body_preview:
The CCLUTIL_WHERE report returns Discern Explorer Programs or EKModules (objects) that use items or commands. By default, CCLUTIL_WHERE performs a TRANSLATE with XML on objects that match a type and name entered at the prompts. title: CEIL Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CEIL( ) function allows you to return the largest integer of a real number. This is a rounding function. The real_expr is rounded up to the next highest whole number.

## Syntax

CEIL(real_expr)

## Metasymbol

_real_expr_
An expression that is a real number.

## Examples

In this example, if X = 5.7, then Y = 6.

Y = CEIL(X)

MPAGES_SVC_EVENT is a routine used to launch into the Discern MPages web service.

**Syntax**

MPAGES_SVC_EVENT(uri,params)

**Metasymbols**

_uri_

An address specifying the report to be executed.

_params_

The parameters for the report.

**Examples**

The following...

window.external.MPAGESOVERRIDEPRINT is a Discern method that changes the behavior of the page when a print event is received. The methods CreateAPViewer(), AppendAPEvent() LaunchAPViewer() are used to set up and display the Pathology Viewer.

The AP Result Viewer is capable of displaying events with an event class code of "MDOC" that have a parent event class code of "AP".

## Syntax

* **CreateAPViewer**()
* **AppendAPEvent**(eventId, parentEventId)
* **LaunchAPViewer**()

**Notes**

The methods must be called in this order.

prompt
"Output to File/Printer/MINE" = "MINE"
with OUTDEV

select into $outdev
from dummyt d
detail
